# Stale leader falls behind, loses election, gets its log truncated when the
# new leader replicates to it.
#
# Four voters, no witness. Quorum = 3.

setup
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1]
v3: [a1]
v4: [a1]
----
v1: [a1] term=a
v2: [a1] term=a
v3: [a1] term=a
v4: [a1] term=a

# v1 wins election.
campaign v1
----
v1 campaigns at term b
v1: yes (self)
v2:
  - term advance a -> b
  - granted vote for v1 at term b
  yes (term a->b, voted v1)
v3:
  - term advance a -> b
  - granted vote for v1 at term b
  yes (term a->b, voted v1)
v4:
  - term advance a -> b
  - granted vote for v1 at term b
  yes (term a->b, voted v1)
outcome: won

# v1 proposes two entries but does NOT replicate them.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b
v3: [a1] term=b
v4: [a1] term=b

propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b
v3: [a1] term=b
v4: [a1] term=b

# v2 campaigns. v1 is dropped (partitioned). v2 wins with v3+v4.
# v1 is not contacted so it stays at term b, still believing it's leader.
campaign v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 campaigns at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
v3:
  - term advance b -> c
  - granted vote for v2 at term c
  yes (term b->c, voted v2)
v4:
  - term advance b -> c
  - granted vote for v2 at term c
  yes (term b->c, voted v2)
outcome: won

# v2 proposes an entry.
propose v2
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b leader
v2: [a1 c2 c3] term=c leader
v3: [a1] term=c
v4: [a1] term=c

# v2 appends to v3 only. Matches: [0, 3, 3, 0]. Quorum match = 0, not committed.
append v2 drop=(v1, v4)
----
v2 appends through c3
v1: (dropped)
v3:
  - accepted append through c3 (ok)
  ok (log=[a1 | c2 c3])
v4: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b leader
v2: [a1 c2 c3] term=c leader
v3: [a1 c2 c3] term=c
v4: [a1] term=c

# v2 appends to v3 and v4. Matches: [0, 3, 3, 3]. Quorum match = 3, committed.
append v2 drop=(v1)
----
v2 appends through c3
v1: (dropped)
v3:
  - accepted append through c3 (ok)
  ok
v4:
  - accepted append through c3 (ok)
  ok (log=[a1 | c2 c3])
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b leader
v2: [a1 c2 c3] term=c leader
v3: [a1 c2 c3] term=c
v4: [a1 c2 c3] term=c

# v1 prevotes to check if it could win. Because it's at a stale term,
# its votes get rejected.
prevote v1
----
v1 prevotes at term c
v1: yes (self)
v2:
  - rejected vote for v1: already voted for v2
  no, already voted for v2
v3:
  - rejected vote for v1: already voted for v2
  no, already voted for v2
v4:
  - rejected vote for v1: already voted for v2
  no, already voted for v2
outcome: lost

# Bump v1 to term c (i.e. pretend it had some contact with the leader but is
# now trying for the subsequent term) so it can try a proper prevote.
set-term v1 c
----
ok

# Now the rejection is on log up-to-date: v2/v3/v4's logs (ending at c3) are
# ahead of v1's (ending at b4) since term c > b.
prevote v1
----
v1 prevotes at term d
v1: yes (self)
v2:
  - term advance c -> d
  - stepped down as leader
  - rejected vote for v1: log not up to date (c3 > b4)
  no, log not up to date (c3 > b4)
v3:
  - term advance c -> d
  - rejected vote for v1: log not up to date (c3 > b4)
  no, log not up to date (c3 > b4)
v4:
  - term advance c -> d
  - rejected vote for v1: log not up to date (c3 > b4)
  no, log not up to date (c3 > b4)
outcome: lost

# v2 appends to everyone including v1. v1's divergent log [b2 b3 b4] gets
# truncated and replaced with v2's log. v1 recognizes v2 as leader and bumps
# to term c.
append v2
----
v2 appends through c3
v1:
  - accepted append through c3 (ok, replaced 3 entries)
  ok, replaced 3 entries (log=[a1 | c2 c3])
v3:
  - accepted append through c3 (ok)
  ok
v4:
  - accepted append through c3 (ok)
  ok
v1: [a1 c2 c3] term=c
v2: [a1 c2 c3] term=c committed=c3 leader
v3: [a1 c2 c3] term=c
v4: [a1 c2 c3] term=c
